pi_items.source: Zotero/Jurism documentation and USGS citation examples. USGS topographic maps are cataloged as 'Map' item type in bibliographic software.
USGS catalog, Data.gov, ScienceBase, and topoView. The Washington West quadrangle is the official name of this 7.5-minute USGS topographic map covering Washington D.C., Maryland, and Virginia.
USGS US Topo availability. Multiple versions of the Washington West 7.5-minute map are available including 2011, 2014, 2016, and 2019 editions. The 2016 version is 35.3 MB PDF.
USGS topographic map specifications. The 7.5-minute series is standardized at 1:24,000 scale. This scale has been used for USGS quadrangle maps since 1947.
USGS Topographic Maps program. US Topo maps (2009-present) are based on the historical 7.5-minute (1:24,000 scale) topographic map series produced by USGS from 1947 to 2006.
USGS official website and map citation standards. The U.S. Geological Survey is the publisher of all official topographic maps in the United States.
USGS Headquarters location. The U.S. Geological Survey National Center is located at 12201 Sunrise Valley Drive, Reston, VA 20192. USGS publications are published from Reston, VA.

USGS citation standards and cartographic citation guides. For USGS sources, the author/creator is 'U.S. Geological Survey' or 'United States Geological Survey'. Multiple authoritative citation sources confirm this naming convention.

USGS employment and topographic map creation. USGS employs cartographers and has published 'Cartographer' as an official job classification (GS-1370). Cartographers are responsible for developing geospatial data and map generation.

privileged_info_summary: The Washington West, DC-MD-VA quadrangle is a real, publicly available USGS topographic map from the 7.5-minute series (1:24,000 scale). A 2016 version exists and can be downloaded as a 35.3 MB PDF from USGS sources. The map is part of the US Topo program featuring geospatial data including orthoimagery, roads, elevation contours, and hydrography. The U.S. Geological Survey is both the publisher and creator (with cartographer role) of this map, headquartered in Reston, Virginia. All metadata values in the task are verified against USGS official sources, citation standards, and public data repositories.
